title: The Free Energy Principle & Active Inference: a Systematic Literature Analysis

creators.name: Knight, Virginia Bleu
creators.affiliation: Active Inference Institute, New Mexico State University, Las Cruces NM. Department of Biology

creators.name: Cordes, RJ
creators.affiliation: Cognitive Security and Education Forum (COGSEC), Active Inference Institute

creators.name: Friedman, Daniel
creators.affiliation: Active Inference Institute, Cognitive Security and Education Forum (COGSEC), University of California, Davis. Department of Entomology & Nematology

description: Here we perform a literature analysis of publications in scientific literature using the term “Free Energy Principle” or “Active Inference”, with an emphasis on works written by Karl J Friston. For a subset of papers with accessible full texts, we performed manual annotation (related to structural, visual, and mathematical features) and automated analyses (related to the terms in the Active Inference Institute’s Active Inference Ontology). The initial analysis here, at the scale of thousands of citations and hundreds of annotated papers, is presented as a first step towards the development of systems which could:  Encompass increased scope of relevant works, including non-textual Integrate multiple forms of annotation and participation  Facilitate integration of manual and artificial contributions Feature richer interfaces for use in learning & research Address field-specific local questions and provide transferable approaches Speak to broader questions in the history and philosophy of science  This project is maintained by the Active Inference Institute. This project has an  interactive Coda site  and a Github repository .

methods.name: Bayesian modeling and inference
methods.description: Applied bayesian modeling and inference approach

key_findings: We perform a systematic literature analysis of publications using the terms 'Free Energy Principle' or 'Active Inference', with emphasis on works by Karl Friston.
